title: PetrVey/pyErosivity
description:
Python package for computing rainfall erosivity (R-factor / EI30) from precipitation time series at any temporal resolution.

Source code and installation: https://github.com/PetrVey/pyErosivity/
Install in editable mode: pip install -e .

What it does: pyErosivity implements the full pipeline from raw precipitation data to per-event and annual erosivity statistics, as required by the USLE/RUSLE family of soil erosion models:

  • Storm separation by minimum dry-spell duration (Wischmeier & Smith 1978)
  • Per-event kinetic energy integration (DIN 19708 / Rogler & Schwertmann 1981; Brown & Foster 1987; McGregor et al. 1995) and peak intensity via sliding-window convolution
  • Erosivity event filtering using the Wischmeier dual criterion (IMax15 ≥ 25.4 mm/h OR accumulated depth ≥ 12.7 mm); alternative IMax30 formulation also supported
  • Optional Renard et al. (1997) within-storm splitting rule
  • Threshold calibration for coarser temporal resolutions (Fischer et al. 2018), correcting the underestimation of peak intensities at hourly resolution
  • Three scaling factor approaches: annual R-factor (SF-R), per-event EI (SF-EI), and climatological mean (SF-clim) for datasets covering different calendar periods
  • Bootstrap uncertainty estimation for annual erosivity, with support for shared sample indices across climate model ensembles

Supports both European (DIN 19708) and US (RUSLE) erosivity standards. Cite all versions using DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20485889.
